description.task: Build a simplified @RestController dispatcher that routes HTTP requests to the correct handler methods based on method and path annotations.
instructions: In dispatch(), iterate all methods of controller via reflection.
For each method, check for the annotation matching httpMethod (GET -> @GetMapping, POST -> @PostMapping, DELETE -> @DeleteMapping).
If the annotation's value() matches path, invoke the method. Pass args[0] if args is not empty.
description.hint: Spring's DispatcherServlet and HandlerMapping work similarly — they scan @RequestMapping annotations on @Controller classes and dispatch requests to the right method.
description.expectedOutput:
[Java, Spring, Docker]
Book 'Kubernetes' added
[Java, Spring, Docker, Kubernetes]
All books removed
404: No handler found for GET /authors

description.task: Implement a path variable extractor that parses URL templates with {variables} and extracts actual values from real URLs.
instructions: In extract(), convert the template to a regex pattern by replacing {varName} with named groups (?<varName>[^/]+). Then match actualPath against this regex.
If matched, extract all named groups and return them as a map. If no match, return empty map.
Implement matches() using the same pattern generation.
description.hint: Spring's @PathVariable extracts URL segments: @GetMapping('/users/{id}') + @PathVariable int id works because Spring uses regex internally to parse path templates.

description.task: Implement a query string parser that extracts and binds query parameters from a URL to named parameters, similar to Spring's @RequestParam.
description.context: Spring's @RequestParam binds query strings like ?page=1&size=10 to method parameters. This is essential for filtering, pagination, and search in REST APIs.
instructions: Implement parse() by splitting on '&', then each part on '=' to build the map.
Handle empty string input by returning an empty map.
Output should show filtered and paginated article results.
description.hint: In Spring: @GetMapping('/articles') + @RequestParam(defaultValue='0') int page. Spring auto-converts query params to method parameter types.
description.expectedOutput:
All (page 0, size 3): [Java Guide, Spring Boot Tutorial, Docker Basics]
Search 'java': [Java Guide]
Search 'spring': [Spring Boot Tutorial]
